definition: A sustained period where prices are generally rising and investor sentiment is optimistic.
A broad market decline, often defined as a fall of 20% or more from recent highs.
A price level where buying pressure has historically been strong enough to halt declines.
A price level where selling pressure has historically been strong enough to halt advances.
When price moves beyond a defined support or resistance area, often with increased volume.
Relative Strength Index, a momentum oscillator ranging from 0 to 100.
Moving Average Convergence Divergence, a trend-following momentum indicator.
A chart style showing open, high, low, and close for each period.
A candlestick pattern where open and close are very close, signaling indecision.
The number of shares or contracts traded during a specific period.
